item.title: The Terrible Cost of the Infinite Scroll
item.description: We are beginning an era in which the social media companies are finally held accountable for their harm.

item.title: It’s Elon Musk’s World Now
item.description: Is Muskism the new Fordism?

item.title: Trump on the Dollar: Dismay and Derision
item.description: Reaction to President Trump’s signature on the dollar. Also: Innocent lives lost in Iran; Democrats’ brand; young job seekers; subway announcements; calling all teenagers.

item.title: Technology Weakens Our Minds. It’s Time to Resist.
item.description: Just as we changed our thinking around physical fitness, we need to change our attitude toward cognitive fitness.

item.title: Republicans Know This War Is Going Badly
item.description: Never before has America arrived at the threshold of a quagmire so quickly.

item.title: The War in Ukraine Changed the World in Ways We’re Only Starting to Comprehend
item.description: Open-ended military conflict in Europe could, at some point, intersect with open-ended military conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Mamdani’s Long Hours Are What Millennial Leadership Looks Like
item.description: There’s a real advantage to being young and governing: being able to work long hours. But there are potential tradeoffs to Mamdani's all-in management style.

item.title: It Was One of the Cold War’s Greatest Crimes. No One Has Paid a Price.
item.description: No one apart from the Congolese people has ever paid a price for the CIA-backed assassination of Patrice Lumumba.

item.title: Trump Cannot See That the Opposition Is Real
item.description: What happens when a narcissist goes to war.

item.title: How Far Will Trump Go in Iran?
item.description: I asked an Iran hawk what victory looks like now.

item.title: How We Stopped a Bid to Seat Election Deniers in Fulton County
item.description: Our local dispute should serve as a warning to all Americans about the midterms.

item.title: Will Iran Break Trumpism?
item.description: The right-wing thinker Christopher Caldwell believes the Iran war is the end of Trumpism. Is he right? What was Trumpism in the first place?

item.title: Trump Said This Policy Would Make Manhattan a ‘Ghost Town.’ He Was Wrong.
item.description: The success of congestion pricing is a reminder that government, done right, has immense power to improve people’s lives.

item.title: It’s Not Trump. It’s America.
item.description: The country’s faith in its ability to shape the world to its liking is a much older malady.

item.title: How to Straighten Out the Hormuz Bottleneck
item.description: Nations are looking for alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: General McChrystal on the Myths Misleading Trump in Iran
item.description: The president may have been seduced by the idea of an easy victory, warns the Iraq and Afghanistan war commander on “The Opinions.”

item.title: The United States Is No Longer the Leader of the Free World
item.description: Pax Americana, meet Lax Americana.

item.title: How Far Will Trump Go in Iran?
item.description: I asked an Iran hawk what victory looks like now.

item.title: Men of the Trump Administration, 2026
item.description: Are Democrats ugly? Asking for a friend.

item.title: Contemporary Opera Doesn’t Need to ‘Challenge’ Audiences
item.description: With a musical language they can appreciate, Americans could more easily find opera’s appeal.

item.title: Memes and Mixed Messages on the Iran War
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by Phil Klay. Also: Educating undocumented students; energy realities; calling all teenagers.

item.title: How Can America Be So Miserable When It’s So Rich?
item.description: The just resentments of a shrinking middle class.

item.title: ‘Everything After This Will Be Harder’: Gen. Stanley McChrystal on Iran
item.description: David French talks with the retired general about the “great seduction” America fell for in Iran.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz Closed Because Iran Is Using the U.S. Playbook
item.description: Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz by raising risks for ship operators. In doing so, it has taken lessons from American financial policy.

item.title: Zack Polanski on the Rise of Britain’s Green Party
item.description: Zach Polanski of Britain’s left-populist party talks austerity, Labour and the establishment crackup.

item.title: There’s a Reason Air Travel Is Such a Mess
item.description: There's a deeper story behind the turbulence in the airline industry.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Brute Force Imperialism’
item.description: From saber rattling over Greenland to war with Iran, the Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie argues, treating the world like a game of Risk has left America in its weakest position since World War II.

item.title: This Should Be Trump’s Next Move With Iran
item.description: An oil blockade could pressure Iran. Sanctions relief would do the opposite.

item.title: Why Are So Many Democrats So Far Out of Touch?
item.description: Candidates in safe districts are under no pressure to moderate in order to win.

item.title: American Bombs Won’t Set Iranian Women Free
item.description: A long line of American politicians have seen bombs as a way of shortening Muslim women’s hemlines

item.title: Billionaires and the Erosion of Democracy
item.description: Readers discuss the effects of billionaires on U.S. society and politics. Also: Electric cars; President Trump’s personal ICE; an invitation to teenagers.

item.title: The War Is Going Better Than You Think
item.description: How the fight in Iran compares to past conflicts.

item.title: Get Ready for More Ticketmaster Wars
item.description: Music fans, get ready for more Ticketmaster wars. Binyamin Appelbaum, a Times Opinion writer, explains the Trump administration’s role in the Live Nation-Ticketmaster antitrust settlement and argues that it is siding with corporate interests at the expense of the American concert-goer.

item.title: What the ‘Shy Girl’ Mess Says About the Future of Fiction
item.description: As more A.I. generated writing is unleashed on the world, more readers will question who — or what — has penned their favorite works.

item.title: American Chipmaking Suffers a Blow Under Trump
item.description: The administration is further weakening the country’s position in the critical semiconductor sector.

item.title: Buckle Up, Women. Cars Still Aren’t Built for You.
item.description: In the United States, women are more likely to be injured and die in a car crash than men. Why? After her own crash, the filmmaker Eve Van Dyke dug through the half-century history of auto safety to find out, and uncovered a disturbing reality: Cars were never designed to protect women’s bodies.

item.title: Buckle Up, Women. Cars Still Aren’t Built for You.
item.description: For over half a century, car safety standards have left women’s lives in the rearview.

item.title: For Putin, the War in Iran Changed Everything
item.description: In one swoop, it overturned the conditions for conciliation.

item.title: We Can’t Let Crypto and A.I. Buy the Policies They Want
item.description: We can’t let crypto and A.I. buy the policies they want.

item.title: Iran’s ‘Nuclear’ Option
item.description: How U.S. strategy encouraged mutually assured destruction.

item.title: The One Question Trump’s Judicial Picks Refuse to Answer
item.description: “Who won the 2020 election?” is the question that Trump’s nominees to the federal bench each refuse to answer in the same exact way.

item.title: Vaccine Advances and the Prism of History
item.description: Readers respond to “I Grew Up Unvaccinated. I’m Lucky I Didn’t Get Sick,” a guest essay by Dr. Elisabeth Marnik. Also: A voice from Norway.

item.title: The Deadly Gender Gap in Car Safety
item.description: For over half a century, car safety standards have left women’s lives in the rearview.

item.title: What Happens if 20 Percent of the World’s Oil Disappears?
item.description: The war with Iran could change how the whole world thinks about energy security in the future. The energy policy expert Jason Bordoff explains.

item.title: Robert Mueller’s Death, and Trump’s Gloating
item.description: Readers respond to Robert Mueller’s death and to President Trump’s coarse reaction. Also: Telephone history; car talk.

item.title: Dyslexia Is Gavin Newsom’s Superpower
item.description: Donald Trump’s insults always say more about him than the people he is trying to mock.
